name: Dick Gregory
description: American comedian, social activist, social critic, writer, and entrepreneur (1932–2017)
abstract: Richard Claxton Gregory (October 12, 1932 – August 19, 2017) was an American comedian, actor, writer, activist and social critic. Gregory became popular among the African-American communities in the southern United States with his "no-holds-barred" sets, poking fun at the bigotry and racism in the United States.

about_quotes.speaker: Percy Heath
about_quotes.evidence: Miles was there in a theater and Dick Gregory was the MC. He told some jokes, funny guy, and he said, “I’m looking for an agent,” and I said him, “Call Monte Kay, our manager.”
